description: Name of the server shown in the server browser list.
Short server description. Shown in the details panel of the server list and printed in chat when a player connects.
example: Role playing server. Reset every Monday. Be nice.

description: UDP port for game traffic. Must be open in your firewall and forwarded on your router.
UDP port for the Steam server browser. Must also be open and forwarded for the server to appear in server lists.
Bind the server to a specific IP address. Leave empty to bind to all available interfaces.
A secondary port used if the primary Port fails or becomes unavailable.
When listing on the EOS server list, the server IP is not shown. Players connect via EOS relay servers instead.
Maximum number of concurrent players on the server. The technical maximum supported is 128.
Maximum number of admins allowed to connect even when the server is full.
Minimum number of free slots that must be available before a new non-admin player can connect. 0 means players can join right up to the slot limit.
Target frames per second for the server tick rate. Higher values increase CPU usage.
Number of AI entity updates processed per server frame. Controls how many enemies and NPCs have their behavior evaluated each tick.
Run the server at a reduced framerate when no players are connected, saving CPU resources.
The framerate target used when LowerFPSWhenEmpty is active.
Server password. Leave empty for a public server.
Enable VAC (Valve Anti-Cheat) protection. VAC-banned clients cannot connect.
Enable Steam authentication for connecting players.
Register the server on the Steam server list.
Register on the Epic Online Services (EOS) list. The game client searches EOS by default.
Epic Online Services log verbosity level. -1 uses the EOS default.
Host as a private session not visible in public server lists. Only invited players can access it.
Enable LAN mode. The server will only be visible on the local network.
Name of the save file directory. Must be a valid folder name with no spaces or special characters.
Override the save file name within the save directory. Leave empty to use the default naming.
Total number of auto saves to keep. The oldest saves are deleted when this limit is exceeded. Works alongside AutoSaveSmartKeep.
Interval in seconds between each automatic save.
Random variance in seconds added to AutoSaveInterval each cycle. Helps stagger saves across multiple servers.

Smart save retention policy. Keeps saves distributed over time rather than just the most recent N. Format: comma-separated A:B:C entries where A = minutes-ago window, B = newest saves to keep, C = oldest saves to keep. Leave empty to use AutoSaveCount only.
Compress save files to reduce disk space usage.
notes: Disabling compression may improve server performance during saves at the cost of larger save file sizes.

description: Name of a ServerGameSettings preset to load. Presets are found in VRisingServer_Data/StreamingAssets/GameSettingPresets/. Leave empty to use your custom ServerGameSettings.json.
Name of a GameDifficulty preset to load. Presets override the GameDifficulty setting in ServerGameSettings. Found in VRisingServer_Data/StreamingAssets/GameDifficultyPresets/.
Override the game server branch/version to run. Examples: legacy-0.5.x, legacy-0.6.x, default.
Days between scheduled server resets/wipes. On startup the server checks if enough time has passed and starts a fresh save if so, backing up the old one. Set to 0 to disable.
Constrains automatic resets to a specific day of the week. Requires ResetDaysInterval to be set. Use 'Any' (-1) to reset based purely on the interval.
Shut down the server after an automatic reset/wipe occurs.
Time in seconds to hold a reconnect slot for a recently disconnected player, allowing them to rejoin without being displaced.
Number of reconnect slots to reserve for recently disconnected players.
Enable a connection queue so players can wait for a free slot when the server is full.
Maximum number of players that can be queued waiting for a server slot.
Interval in seconds between queue position updates sent to waiting players.
Restrict debug console events to admin players only.
Disable all debug console events entirely.
Grant all connected players full admin privileges.
Enable debug events that could disrupt normal server operation or expose sensitive information.
Grant starter items to newly created characters. Likely paired with StarterEquipmentId in ServerGameSettings.
URL to a remote ban list. The server downloads and applies bans from this URL on startup.
URL to a remote temporary ban list.
URL to a remote admin list. The server downloads and applies admin permissions from this URL on startup.
URL to a remote chat mute list.
URL to a remote voice mute list.
Automatically teleport players who get stuck in geometry or collide with objects in unintended ways.
Fixed UTC offset in minutes for the server clock. The default value (int minimum) means use the system's local timezone.
Determines the AFK kick mode. Disabled = never kick; Enabled = always kick after AFKKickDuration; EnabledWhenPlayerRatioReached = kick only when AFKKickPlayerRatio of slots are in use.
How long in seconds a player can be AFK before being kicked.
Duration in seconds before the AFK kick that a warning is sent to the player.
The ratio of active players required to trigger AFK kicks when using EnabledWhenPlayerRatioReached mode. 0.5 means AFK kick activates when at least 50% of player slots are in use.
Log all network activity to the server log.
Log actions and commands performed by server administrators.
Log debug messages generated during server operation.
Automatically run data integrity tests on save files whenever the world is saved.
Generate and save a summary of the game world state alongside each save file.
Save additional debug information alongside each regular save file.
Manually override the persistence/save format version. -1 uses the default version.
Fixed time offset added to the server start time.
Log archetype creation events during server startup.
Enable Backtrace ANR (Application Not Responding) tracking for crash diagnostics.
Enable analytics data collection for Stunlock Studios.
Analytics environment target. prod sends to the production pipeline.
Enable debug logging for the analytics system.
Enable action records logging.
Use a double transport layer for network communication.
Enable RCON remote console access. A password must be set when enabling.
TCP port for RCON connections.
Password required to authenticate RCON connections. Cannot be empty when RCON is enabled.
Bind the RCON socket to a specific IP address. Overrides the global Address setting for RCON. Useful when binding to a separate internal interface.
Seconds of inactivity before an RCON connection is dropped.
Maximum number of failed password attempts before an RCON client is banned.
Minutes to ban an RCON client after exceeding MaxPasswordTries. 0 = no ban.
Send authentication response immediately without waiting for a follow-up request.
Maximum simultaneous RCON connections from a single IP address.
Maximum total simultaneous RCON connections.
Enable experimental RCON commands not yet in stable support.
Enable the server HTTP API endpoint.
IP address the API server binds to. '*' binds to all available interfaces.
TCP port for the HTTP API server.
Base URL path prefixed to all API endpoints.
Comma-separated list of IP addresses or CIDR ranges permitted to access the API. Leave empty to allow all connections.
Interval in seconds between Prometheus metrics scrapes.
